plants.name: Chili
plants.description: Spicy peppers ideal for tropical climates. Thrives in hot and humid conditions.
plants.care_tips: Water deeply but infrequently. Ensure well-draining soil.

plants.name: Mint
plants.description: Fast-growing aromatic herb. Can grow invasively so container planting is recommended.
plants.care_tips: Keep soil consistently moist. Pinch flowers to encourage leaf growth.

plants.name: Lettuce
plants.description: Cool-season leafy green. Ideal for indoor hydroponic or container setups.
plants.care_tips: Avoid temperatures above 28°C or it will bolt. Harvest outer leaves regularly.

plants.name: Basil
plants.description: Classic culinary herb with a sweet, peppery aroma. Perfect for cooking and companion planting.
plants.care_tips: Pinch off flower buds to extend leaf production. Loves 6+ hours of sun.

plants.name: Cherry Tomato
plants.description: Compact tomato variety ideal for containers. Produces abundantly over a long harvest period.
plants.care_tips: Support with stakes. Feed weekly with fertiliser once flowering begins.

plants.name: Kangkung (Water Spinach)
plants.description: Fast-growing tropical leafy vegetable. A staple in Southeast Asian cuisine. Thrives in heat and moisture.
plants.care_tips: Can be grown in a container with a tray of water. Harvest frequently to stimulate regrowth.

plants.name: Pandan
plants.description: Fragrant tropical herb essential in Southeast Asian cooking. Used in rice, desserts, and drinks.
plants.care_tips: Keep soil moist but not soggy. Thrives in humid, tropical climates like Malaysia.

plants.name: Calamansi Lime
plants.description: Miniature citrus fruit widely used in Southeast Asian cooking and drinks. Great for container growing.
plants.care_tips: Feed with citrus fertiliser monthly. Ensure good drainage. Prune after harvest season.

plants.name: Rosemary
plants.description: Fragrant Mediterranean herb used for cooking and aromatherapy. Attracts pollinators.
plants.care_tips: Needs excellent drainage and air circulation. Avoid overwatering — the main cause of death.

plants.name: Butterfly Pea
plants.description: Vibrant blue flowers used to make natural food colouring and tea. Fast-growing tropical vine.
plants.care_tips: Train along a trellis or fence. Harvest flowers regularly to encourage continuous blooming.

plants.name: Lady Finger (Okra)
plants.description: Heat-tolerant vegetable that thrives in tropical conditions. Produces abundantly through the year.
plants.care_tips: Harvest pods at 7–10 cm length for best tenderness. Larger pods become tough and fibrous.

plants.name: Curry Leaf
plants.description: Aromatic herb widely used in South and Southeast Asian cooking. Leaves release fragrance when touched.
plants.care_tips: Do not overwater. Apply iron-rich fertiliser if leaves yellow. Grows slowly initially.

plants.name: Sunflower
plants.description: Cheerful, uplifting flowers that attract bees and birds. Seeds are edible and nutritious.
plants.care_tips: Plant facing south or southwest. Stake taller varieties. Seeds can be harvested when the back turns brown.

plants.name: Spring Onion
plants.description: One of the easiest vegetables to regrow from kitchen scraps. A continuous harvest staple.
plants.care_tips: Regrow from cut bases in a glass of water, then transplant to soil. Keep soil slightly moist.

plants.name: Lemon Grass
plants.description: Tropical grass with a citrus aroma, essential in many Asian dishes. Also a natural mosquito repellent.
plants.care_tips: Harvest outer stalks when the clump is established. Divide clumps every 2 years.

plants.name: Brinjal (Eggplant)
plants.description: Heat-loving vegetable with purple fruits. Widely used in Malaysian and Southeast Asian cooking.
plants.care_tips: Stake the plant when fruits develop. Keep soil consistently moist to prevent bitter fruits.

plants.name: Ulam Raja
plants.description: Popular Malaysian salad herb eaten raw. Known for its antioxidant properties and rapid growth.
plants.care_tips: A true tropical plant — thrives with minimal care in Malaysian conditions. Self-seeds readily.

plants.name: Microgreens
plants.description: Nutrient-dense seedlings harvested 7–14 days after germination. Ideal for windowsill growing year-round.
plants.care_tips: Mist twice daily. Harvest with scissors just above the soil line when first true leaves appear.

plants.name: Sweet Potato Leaves
plants.description: Nutritious leafy vegetable widely eaten in Southeast Asia. Leaves and shoots are harvested young and stir-fried.
plants.care_tips: Harvest young shoot tips regularly to keep the plant bushy and productive. Very drought-tolerant once established.

plants.name: Ginger
plants.description: Tropical rhizome used extensively in Malaysian and Asian cooking, teas, and traditional medicine. Thrives in warm, humid conditions.
plants.care_tips: Plant rhizome just below the surface. Keep consistently moist and shaded from harsh afternoon sun.

plants.name: Turmeric
plants.description: Golden rhizome with powerful anti-inflammatory properties. A staple spice in Malaysian and Indian cuisine.
plants.care_tips: Plant rhizomes horizontally 5 cm deep. Mulch well to retain moisture. Harvest after leaves yellow and die back.

plants.name: Long Bean
plants.description: Fast-producing tropical bean with pods up to 60 cm long. A popular vegetable in Malaysian home cooking.
plants.care_tips: Provide a trellis or stakes for climbing. Harvest pods when young and tender before they become stringy.

plants.name: Bitter Gourd (Bitter Melon)
plants.description: Distinctive bitter-tasting vegetable prized in Malaysian and Chinese cuisine. Known for its blood sugar-regulating properties.
plants.care_tips: Train on a trellis. Harvest fruits when still green and firm for best flavour. Regular harvesting increases yield.

plants.name: Banana
plants.description: Iconic tropical fruit that grows vigorously in Malaysian conditions. Young flowers (jantung pisang) and leaves are also edible.
plants.care_tips: Plant in a sunny spot with rich soil. Remove all but one or two suckers to focus energy on fruit production.

plants.name: Pineapple
plants.description: Tropical fruit grown from the crown of a store-bought pineapple. Drought-tolerant and surprisingly easy to grow.
plants.care_tips: Root the crown in water before planting. Allow soil to dry between waterings. Fruit production begins after 18 months.

plants.name: Papaya
plants.description: Fast-growing tropical fruit tree that can produce fruit within 9 months. Young green papaya is also used in cooking.
plants.care_tips: Grows quickly from seed. Ensure excellent drainage — standing water kills the roots. Protect from strong winds.

plants.name: Torch Ginger (Bunga Kantan)
plants.description: Striking ornamental ginger with edible flower buds used in Malaysian cuisine, especially in laksa and rojak.
plants.care_tips: Loves moisture and partial shade. Harvest flower buds before they fully open for best flavour.

plants.name: Moringa (Drumstick Tree)
plants.description: Nutrient-dense superfood tree with edible leaves, pods, flowers, and seeds. Grows rapidly in tropical climates.
plants.care_tips: Extremely drought-tolerant once established. Prune regularly to keep it bushy and within reach for harvesting.

plants.name: Pegaga (Centella)
plants.description: Creeping medicinal herb eaten raw as ulam in Malaysia. Rich in antioxidants and widely used in traditional herbal drinks.
plants.care_tips: Loves damp conditions — ideal near water features or in a tray of water. Harvest outer leaves regularly.

plants.name: Kaffir Lime
plants.description: Citrus tree prized for its aromatic double leaves and zesty rind, both essential in Malaysian and Thai cooking.
plants.care_tips: Feed monthly with citrus fertiliser. Watch for leaf miners. Harvest leaves as needed — they regrow readily.
